response:
## What `src/billing.ts` does

A 310-line billing module that ships two main responsibilities:

- **`generateInvoice` (lines 50–253, 204 lines)** — the workhorse invoice builder. Pulls plan/customer state, stamps timestamps via `Date.now()`/`new Date()` in 7 spots, and likely composes line items. The size (3.4× the 60-line domain threshold) suggests it bundles unrelated steps.
- **`calculateRefundPreview` (lines 40–44)** — name implies a pure preview, but it actually calls `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail`. Treat it as a side-effecting operation, not a calculator.
- A comment at line 39 carries a hidden authorization rule: *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves"* — not visibly encoded in nearby code.
- The file mentions `"enterprise"` as a bare string and has 4 stray TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX markers (lines 183–308).

## Files I'd also read before editing

- **`src/billing.test.ts`** — existing coverage is weak (one test with zero asserts, one with weak matchers), so don't trust it to catch regressions.
- **`src/api/plan.ts`, `src/jobs/plan-sync.ts`, `src/ui/pricing.ts`** — the `"enterprise"` literal lives in all four; a change to plan semantics likely needs touching them.
- **`src/date.ts`** — if I want to fix temporal coupling here, the shared clock/abstraction should live there (and that file has its own Date crimes: mixed UTC/local, DST-naive math, unsafe parses).
- **`src/routes/settings/billing.tsx`, `src/nav/registry.ts`, `src/nav/sidebar.ts`** — billing route is labelled inconsistently ("Plans" vs "Billing" vs `PricingPage` vs `<title>Subscription`); changing user-facing wording requires aligning these.
- **`docs/billing.md`** — concept drift between *tenant/account/organisation/team/workspace* aliases is documented here.

## Risks flagged for `src/billing.ts`

| ID | Charge | Severity | Where |
|---|---|---|---|
| crime_00002 | God Function (`generateInvoice`, 204 lines) | high | L50–253 |
| crime_00009 | Temporal Recklessness (7× Date) | medium | L57, 63, 92, 198, 238, 250, 273 |
| crime_00013 | False Identity (`calculateRefundPreview` side effects) | medium | L40–44 |
| crime_00016 | God File (310 lines, threshold 300) | medium | whole file |
| crime_00017 | Logic in the Alibi (auth rule in a comment) | medium | L39 |
| crime_00012 | String Sprinkles (`"enterprise"` scattered in 4 files) | medium | shared |
| crime_00035 | Unfinished Business (4 TODO/FIXME markers) | low | L183–308 |

Practical implication for an edit: any change touching invoice generation, refund flow, or the enterprise plan branch should (a) be done in a new helper rather than growing `generateInvoice`, (b) thread a clock/now() argument rather than reading wall time inline, and (c) make the owner/billing-support rule from the line-39 comment into a named guard with a test — the existing test file won't catch it breaking.
